title: Elevating Natural Gas Liquid (NGLs) Extraction and Fractionation Train Performance: A Synergistic Approach of Simulation Analysis, Advanced Modification, and Vertical Advancement
author: Nnadikwe, Johnson
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This study addresses the research gap in optimizing Natural Gas Liquid (NGL) extraction and fractionation train performance, where energy consumption remains a significant challenge. We integrated simulation analysis, advanced modifications, and technological advancements to enhance efficiency, examining various Heat Pump Aided Distillation (HPAD) and Self-Heat Recuperation Technology (SHRT) options to retrofit a single column. Using Aspen HYSYS V7.3, we simulated and analyzed eight retrofit designs, revealing significant energy savings opportunities. The results show that Mechanical Vapor Re-compression (MVR) achieved the most substantial energy cost reduction of 68.11%, followed by Vapor Compression (VC) with 66.65%, Modified Self-Heat Recuperative (MSHR) with 64.02%, and other notable options. These findings highlight the potential of HPAD and SHRT in reducing energy consumption in NGL fractionation trains, with implications for process improvement and energy efficiency enhancement.

keywords: HYSYS Simulation, Heat, Pump, NGL, Extraction, Distillation
